beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and prepare for the day at home.
beats.reasoning: Henry starts every day with intention. Low energy yesterday (40/100) means he needs a gentle morning—no swim today, just a solid home routine to reset before heading out.

beats.activity: Commute to Beacon Hill Pool for a morning swim.
beats.reasoning: Henry can't start the day without a swim—it's his anchor. Pool is close to home, so this is a short walk. Overcast weather won't stop him; the ritual matters more.

beats.activity: Swim and decompress in the water.
beats.reasoning: A full 55 minutes of swimming to shake off yesterday's lingering buzz and low energy. Saturday morning pool is quiet—exactly what he needs for clarity.

beats.activity: Head home to change and grab coffee.
beats.reasoning: Short walk home to shower off pool water and transition into the day. Frugal Henry makes his own coffee at home rather than buying out.

beats.activity: Coffee and light breakfast at home, check phone.
beats.reasoning: A few minutes to ground himself, caffeinate, and decide how the day unfolds. Saturday is unstructured—perfect for mood-based planning.

beats.activity: Walk to Beacon Hill farmers market to pick up fresh produce.
beats.location_name: Beacon Hill Farmers Market (Beacon Hill, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Overcast Saturday, farmers markets are in full swing. Henry's frugal and tends his garden, so sourcing local produce is both practical and joyful. Staying in neighborhood keeps it low-key.

beats.activity: Walk back home with market haul.
beats.reasoning: Short walk home with groceries. Henry values simplicity and staying close to base when energy is still recovering.

beats.activity: Tend to garden beds, prep vegetables, organize produce.
beats.reasoning: Urban gardening is his decompression. Saturday afternoon is prime time to water, prune, and enjoy the soil work that grounds him after a busy week.

beats.activity: Lunch: simple meal at home with market ingredients.
beats.reasoning: Frugal Henry cooks at home with fresh market finds. Mariners game is at 1pm in SoDo, but he's not a baseball person—overcast Saturday mood favors quiet lunch instead.

beats.activity: Commute to Fremont for the farmers market and neighborhood walk.
beats.reasoning: Across-town journey (Beacon Hill to Fremont ~4 km, 35–40 min by transit/rideshare). Saturday afternoon exploring a different neighborhood's market and energy. Henry values exploration and community vibes.

beats.activity: Browse Fremont farmers market, grab a casual bite, walk around neighborhood.
beats.reasoning: Henry enjoys exploring neighborhoods and street culture. Fremont on Saturday afternoon is eclectic and low-pressure—fits his mood. Grab street food or coffee, soak in the vibe.
Return journey across town (40 min). Evening is settling in; Henry values time with close friends over big crowds, so he's heading home to rest before evening plans.

beats.activity: Relax at home, dinner prep, read, light TV, prepare for bed.
beats.reasoning: Long wind-down evening at home. After yesterday's high energy (Capitol Hill Pride buzz), today is about grounding and recovery. Frugal Henry stays home, cooks, and settles into evening routine.
8.5 hours of restorative sleep. Henry ends the day at home, crossing midnight into Sunday morning. Low energy recovery continues; he needs the rest before the week ahead.

world_event_prompt: Overcast Saturday, high of 64F. Farmers markets in Ballard, Fremont, and the U-District in full swing. Mariners home game at T-Mobile Park at 1pm brings SoDo crowds.
